summary: Closed, unmerged PR that attempted a broad item-system decompilation batch. The PR summary claims 24 item functions across Dosei, Great Fox Laser, Kabigon, Lugia, Ness PK Flush/Thunder Trail/Yoyo, Old Ottosea, Patapata, Pikachu Thunder Jolt Air, Tincle, and White Bea, with ninja, ninja diff, and objdiff 100% matches reported. The diff also added substantial Claude/decomp automation and guidance files, so this was not a pure item-code PR. There were no issue comments, reviews, or review comments in the slice, so reviewer rationale for closure is unavailable.
what_changed: Replaced item stub markers with C implementations for visible functions including it_3F14_Logic7_Thrown, it_3F14_Logic7_DmgDealt, itGreatfoxlaser_UnkMotion1_Phys, it_802C9D40, itKabigon_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_802CA3F4, itLugia_UnkMotion4_Phys, it_2725_Logic102_Destroyed, itNesspkthundertrail_UnkMotion0_Anim, it_802BE5D8, it_802BE958, itOldottosea_UnkMotion7_Coll, it_802E0D9C, it_802B43D0, itTincle_UnkMotion4_Phys, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll, itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim, it_802E3DA0, it_802E4464, and it_2725_Logic9_Dropped.

Updated item headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed prototypes for functions such as it_802CA074(Item_GObj*), it_802D1A44(Item_GObj*), it_802E0D9C(Item_GObj*), and it_802B43D0(Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*).

Expanded item data typing in itCommonItems.h, including new itTincleAttributes, new fields in itLugiaAttributes and itWhiteBeaAttributes, a new x44 field in itWhiteBea_ItemVars, changed itDosei_ItemVars.xDE0 from s32 to f32, and added unions/floating aliases plus xE48 in itPokemon_ItemVars.

Added includes needed by the decompiled bodies, including ef/efsync.h, gr/stage.h, baselib/random.h, baselib/jobj.h, baselib/gobjplink.h, fighter-specific Ness/Kirby headers, db/db.h, mp/mpcoll.h, and mp/mplib.h.
Added large decompilation automation/tooling files: scripts/decomp_helpers.py, scripts/decomp_overnight.sh, scripts/find_stubs.py, scripts/find_all_stubs.sh, and scripts/schedule_overnight.sh.
Added CLAUDE.md with repository build commands, style rules, naming conventions, decompilation workflow, matching notes, and permuter guidance.
smart_moves: Converted several unknown prototypes to concrete Item_GObj/HSD_GObj signatures, which makes later item decompilation safer and reduces reliance on UNK_PARAMS.
Improved item variable and attribute structs rather than hiding accesses behind raw pointer arithmetic; examples include adding typed White Bea, Tincle, Lugia, and Pokemon fields.
Used existing item helper/state functions instead of open-coded state transitions, e.g. Item_80268E5C, Item_8026AE84, it_8026B3A8, it_80272940, it_80275258, and collision helpers.
Preserved matching-oriented stack adjustments with PAD_STACK(8) in functions where the source shape apparently needed stack-size control.
Modeled item-owner cleanup paths explicitly for Ness/Kirby PK Flash and Yoyo ownership interactions using fighter-specific helper calls instead of leaving lifecycle behavior opaque.
For Kabigon random effect spawning, introduced a local typed attributes struct and used HSD_Randf with efSync_Spawn, making the randomized positional offset readable.
decomp_lessons: For item files, decompiling one function often forces header and struct improvements; capture those type discoveries in itCommonItems.h or the item header instead of keeping local UNK access.
When an item uses xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes, adding a small local attributes struct can clarify repeated field accesses and avoid fake matches.
Union aliases in item variable structs can preserve existing integer uses while enabling float-typed decompilation when the same storage is used as f32 in a newly decompiled function.

State callbacks with names like Logic*_Dropped, Logic*_Destroyed, UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll should usually keep the item-system signature shape: Item_GObj* argument and bool return for animation/collision callbacks.
Use PAD_STACK only as a matching tactic when needed; it appears in several bodies here and should remain a last-mile matching tool rather than a substitute for correct control flow.
Broad decomp batches mixed with unrelated automation/docs can be hard to review or merge; future work should consider separating item C/header decomp changes from Claude/tooling changes.
naming_conventions: CLAUDE.md states module prefixes such as it=item, ft=fighter, gr=ground/stage, gm=game, lb=library, cm=camera, ef=effect, mp=map/collision, and db=debug.
CLAUDE.md states functions are prefixed with their file or module name, with examples ftCo_Attack100_Enter, lbVector_sin, and ftFox_LaserOnDeath.
CLAUDE.md states file names use lowerCamelCase and function names use UpperCamelCase, while standard-library reimplementations use lowercase.

Struct fields in the added guidance are expected to carry hex-offset naming/comments such as /* 0x10 */ u8 x10_thing; the item struct edits follow this style partially with x44/x48 fields and offset comments.
Changed item callback names retain existing project conventions such as itWhitebea_UnkMotion1_Anim, itTincle_UnkMotion7_Coll, and it_2725_Logic9_Dropped.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body reports successful ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff matches for all targeted functions, but the slice contains no independent reviewer verification.
Several functions use PAD_STACK(8), indicating stack-size matching adjustments were needed after otherwise readable C was written.

The added CLAUDE.md recommends true matches over fake pointer-arithmetic matches, avoiding raw pointer arithmetic and goto, rewriting simple do/while patterns as for loops, ignoring pure register swaps until permuter work, and using ABS/MIN/MAX macros for common patterns.
The added CLAUDE.md recommends objdiff with relaxed relocation diffs and decomp-permuter when only register allocation or small source-shape differences remain.
The decompiled functions generally use typed Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj) or gobj->user_data, local attribute pointers, and existing helper calls to shape code close to engine idioms.
review_feedback: No review comments, reviews, or issue comments were present in the PR slice.
The PR was closed without merge; the available slice does not state why.
follow_up_queries: Find later merged PRs that reintroduced any item functions from PR #2277.
Check whether CLAUDE.md or scripts/decomp_overnight.sh were later accepted, reverted, or split into separate tooling PRs.
Audit item struct changes from this PR against current itCommonItems.h before reusing field names such as itPokemon_ItemVars.xE48 or itWhiteBea_ItemVars.x44.
Search for later review discussion explaining why PR #2277 was closed unmerged.
